event.message: 已找到 7 个主题方向

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Use broad media-type buckets first: documentation, issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search, mail/chat, and local tools. Do not split those buckets by domain or project during the coarse pass.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Media-type organization. Use page or information shape as the primary axis: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; only split when the group would be too large or pages are clearly different media types.

message.content: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"documentation","title":"Documentation","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10000,10013,10014,10016,10017,10019,10020,10022,10024,10025,10027,10030],"reason":"Docs and guides across MDN, Google Docs, and similar reference pages."},{"bucketKey":"issues_prs","title":"Issues / PRs","color":"red","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10002,10005,10008,10011,10033],"reason":"Issue-tracking and bug discussion pages."},{"bucketKey":"papers","title":"Papers","color":"purple","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10004,10007,10010],"reason":"Research paper and paper-index pages."},{"bucketKey":"videos","title":"Videos","color":"pink","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10009,10012,10015,10034],"reason":"Video or talk pages from streaming platforms."},{"bucketKey":"articles_news","title":"Articles / News","color":"green","confidence":0.88,"tabIds":[10018,10021,10023,10026],"reason":"Long-form reading and article-style pages."},{"bucketKey":"shopping_finance","title":"Shopping / Finance","color":"yellow","confidence":0.85,"tabIds":[10028,10031],"reason":"Purchase and checkout related pages."},{"bucketKey":"dashboards","title":"Dashboards","color":"cyan","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10029,10032,10035],"reason":"Dashboard and leaderboard monitoring views."}],"reviewTabIds":[10001,10003,10006]}

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Runtime preset media_type overrides the default topic axis. Group primarily by page/media type: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; do not split docs, issues, videos, or papers by project/topic unless maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it to identify page/media type from visible content. Do not use page topic from samples to split tabs that share the same media type in media_type mode.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create unrelated generic catch-all groups. In media_type mode, a documentation, issue/PR, paper, video, article, dashboard, shopping/finance, search, mail/chat, or local-tool group is an intentional group, not a catch-all; split only when maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Media-type organization. Use page or information shape as the primary axis: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; only split when the group would be too large or pages are clearly different media types.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: 待分类.
Coarse reason: 粗分阶段认为这些标签页仍不确定。
This is a large-session media-type refinement pass; preserve the media-type axis and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
If this bucket is already one page/media type, return one group with all ids unless maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded.
Split only when the bucket mixes clearly different page/media types, not by project, topic, domain, or contiguous task.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"shopping_finance","title":"Finance / Billing","color":"green","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10003,10006],"reason":"These tabs are billing/account finance portal pages and fit the shopping/finance media type."}],"review":[{"id":10001,"reason":"Design reference image page; media type is less certain in this pass, so review it separately."}]}

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"documentation","title":"文档","color":"blue","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10000,10013,10014,10016,10017,10019,10020,10022,10024,10025,10027,10030],"reason":"这些标签页都属于文档/说明页面，应按媒体类型集中整理，而不是按项目或域名拆分。"},{"key":"issues_prs","title":"问题与工单","color":"red","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10002,10005,10008,10011,10033],"reason":"这些页面都是 issue/工单/缺陷讨论，跨 GitHub 与 Jira 但媒体类型一致，适合放在同一组。"},{"key":"shopping_finance","title":"购物与财务","color":"green","confidence":0.94,"ids":[10003,10006,10028,10031],"reason":"这些标签页都与账单、结算、购买或财务账户相关，属于同一购物/财务类型。"},{"key":"papers","title":"论文","color":"purple","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10004,10007,10010],"reason":"这些页面都是论文或论文索引/配套页面，媒体类型明确，适合合并为论文组。"},{"key":"videos","title":"视频","color":"pink","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10009,10012,10015,10034],"reason":"这些标签页都是视频内容，包括教程和演讲视频，应统一归入视频组。"},{"key":"articles_news","title":"文章","color":"yellow","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10018,10021,10023,10026],"reason":"这些页面都是长文或文章阅读页，虽然主题不同，但在媒体类型模式下应保持为文章组。"},{"key":"dashboards","title":"仪表盘","color":"cyan","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10029,10032,10035],"reason":"这些页面都是看板或排行榜类仪表盘，信息形态一致，适合集中整理。"}],"review":[{"id":10001,"reason":"看起来像设计灵感/图片参考页，不完全符合预设媒体类型，放入复核更稳妥。"}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing document priorities**

I'm thinking about the order of tasks based on their IDs. Maybe I should tackle documents first, followed by issues which is tied to ID 10002. Then I could focus on shopping and finance at 10003, tackle papers at 10004, and address video content at 10009. Articles could follow at 10018, and then dashboards at 10029. I might need to review ID 10001 as well. I really want to ensure that each ID is handled exactly once for confidence.

message.content: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"documentation","title":"Documentation","color":"blue","confidence":0.96,"tabIds":[10000,10013,10014,10016,10017,10019,10020,10022,10024,10025,10027,10030,10038,10039,10041,10042,10045],"reason":"Reference and guide pages across docs sites."},{"bucketKey":"issues_prs","title":"Issues/PRs","color":"red","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10002,10005,10008,10011,10033,10036],"reason":"Issue-tracking and debugging discussion tabs."},{"bucketKey":"papers","title":"Papers","color":"purple","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10004,10007,10010,10044,10047],"reason":"Research paper and benchmark pages."},{"bucketKey":"videos","title":"Videos","color":"pink","confidence":0.97,"tabIds":[10009,10012,10015,10034,10037,10040],"reason":"Video tutorial or talk pages."},{"bucketKey":"articles_news","title":"Articles/News","color":"green","confidence":0.92,"tabIds":[10018,10021,10023,10026,10043,10046],"reason":"Long-form articles, essays, and newsletters."},{"bucketKey":"dashboards","title":"Dashboards","color":"cyan","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10029,10032,10035],"reason":"Dashboard and leaderboard views."},{"bucketKey":"shopping_finance","title":"Shopping/Finance","color":"yellow","confidence":0.88,"tabIds":[10003,10006,10028,10031],"reason":"Billing, account, checkout, and purchase-related pages."},{"bucketKey":"design_reference","title":"Articles/News","color":"green","confidence":0.66,"tabIds":[10001],"reason":"Visual inspiration/reference content fits best with reading material in this coarse pass."}],"reviewTabIds":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Compacting output structure**

I need to produce a precise JSON object, likely in English since the titles are mainly in that language. I should organize it into broad media-type categories. It's important to include all tabs without requiring a review. For sensitive information, like financial accounts, it might fit into the shopping or finance category. Every ID has to be included just once, so I’ll double-check to ensure everything's accounted for accurately!

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"articles_news","title":"Articles/News","color":"blue","confidence":0.92,"ids":[10001],"reason":"Single tab in this refinement pass; visual reference reading content fits the articles/news media-type bucket."}],"review":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing JSON outputs**

I need to output just one JSON object, and it looks like I'm focusing on a coarse bucket for articles or news refinement. I should keep only one tab and maintain the media-type axis. It seems best to group all IDs together and choose a dominant language, probably English. As for the title, I could go with something like "Articles/News" or, if I want to be more specific, just "Articles." Keeping the reasoning brief is essential.

event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Runtime preset media_type overrides the default topic axis. Group primarily by page/media type: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; do not split docs, issues, videos, or papers by project/topic unless maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it to identify page/media type from visible content. Do not use page topic from samples to split tabs that share the same media type in media_type mode.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create unrelated generic catch-all groups. In media_type mode, a documentation, issue/PR, paper, video, article, dashboard, shopping/finance, search, mail/chat, or local-tool group is an intentional group, not a catch-all; split only when maxTabsPerGroup would be exceeded or the pages are clearly different media types.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Media-type organization. Use page or information shape as the primary axis: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; only split when the group would be too large or pages are clearly different media types.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"documentation","title":"Documentation","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10000,10013,10014,10016,10017,10019,10020,10022,10024,10025,10027,10030,10038,10039,10041,10042,10045],"reason":"Documentation pages grouped together across domains per media-type mode."},{"key":"issues_prs","title":"Issues / PRs","color":"red","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10002,10005,10008,10011,10033,10036],"reason":"Issue-tracker threads and bug/task discussions are the same code-work media type."},{"key":"papers","title":"Papers","color":"purple","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10004,10007,10010,10044,10047],"reason":"Research-paper and paper-index pages belong in one papers group."},{"key":"videos","title":"Videos","color":"pink","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10009,10012,10015,10034,10037,10040],"reason":"Video content grouped together across platforms in media-type mode."},{"key":"articles_news","title":"Articles / News","color":"green","confidence":0.93,"ids":[10018,10021,10023,10026,10043,10046],"reason":"Long-form reading and newsletter/article pages are the same article/news media type."},{"key":"shopping_finance","title":"Shopping / Finance","color":"yellow","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10028,10031],"reason":"Purchase and checkout comparison pages fit the shopping/finance media type."},{"key":"dashboards","title":"Dashboards","color":"cyan","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10029,10032,10035],"reason":"Leaderboard and dashboard views are grouped as dashboards across apps."}],"review":[{"id":10001,"reason":"Design reference image page does not fit a clear primary media-type bucket with enough confidence."},{"id":10003,"reason":"Billing/account portal appears sensitive financial/account-related; send to review."},{"id":10006,"reason":"Billing/account portal appears sensitive financial/account-related; send to review."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Considering sensitive pages**

The prompt indicates a need to review low-confidence, generic, or sensitive pages. An "Account page - invoice and billing portal" could be sensitive finance, which means it should go to review. Shopping or finance pages might also be sensitive, like Stripe's invoices. I think account pages, like 10003/10006, should go to review due to sensitive billing info. Meanwhile, titles like docs.google.com aren't sensitive, but I need to consider what each reference could mean carefully.

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Use broad media-type buckets first: documentation, issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search, mail/chat, and local tools. Do not split those buckets by domain or project during the coarse pass.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Media-type organization. Use page or information shape as the primary axis: documentation, code/issues/PRs, papers, videos, articles/news, dashboards, shopping/finance, search results, mail/chat, and local tools. Keep the same media type together across domains and projects; only split when the group would be too large or pages are clearly different media types.

message.reasoning_content:
**Considering visual references**

I might use some articles or news for discussion. I think references 10001 and 10048 can be grouped under "设计参考," but I'm realizing I need to keep everything in English. So, I should ensure that the titles I choose are also in English. This will make it easier for everyone to understand and access the information without any language barriers. I want to make sure everything aligns smoothly!

groups.reason: Tutorial and talk video pages across YouTube and Bilibili should stay together as videos.

groups.title: Articles / News
groups.reason: Long-form essays, newsletters, and article-style reading tabs fit the articles/news media type.

groups.title: Dashboards
groups.reason: Leaderboard and dashboard views are operational dashboard pages and should be grouped together.

reviewTabs.reason: Visual reference image page does not map cleanly to the preset media buckets; review manually.
Figma visual example is design-reference content rather than a clear preset media type; review manually.
Forum discussion is mixed reference/discussion content and is lower-confidence than documentation or article buckets.
